title: 🧩subworkflow - dynatrace export one or all workflows to existing github repo
description:
Subworkflow to export one or all workflows to a github repository

7 parameters:

workflowId: (Optional) id of the workflow to export for example "15508756-7ee1-4c03-ad1b-d3d95aca478f" (if left empty "" all workflows will be exported)
searchLimit: (Required) maximum number of workflow to export
githubRepoName: (Required) Existing Repository name of Github Repo to export to 
githubOwnerName: (Required) Github Onwer Name  
githubRepoBranchName: (Required) Github Branche name of repo to export to
dynatraceGithubConnection: (Required) dynatrace github connection name
adminAccess: (Required) true/false if false only workflows that the user has permissions to will be exported, if true and the user has workflow-admin rights then all workflows will be exported
guide:
## 📤 **Subworkflow to Export One or More Workflows to a GitHub Repository**

📦 This subworkflow provides an automated way to **export one or all workflows to a GitHub repository**.

It supports **workflow-as-code** practices by enabling version control, backup, governance, and promotion of workflows across environments.

---

### 🎯 **Use Case**

Typical use cases include:

- 🗂️ Backing up workflows to GitHub for safekeeping
- 🔁 Promoting workflows between environments (dev → test → prod)
- 🛡️ Supporting governance, audit, and compliance requirements
- 📦 Managing workflows as code using Git-based processes

This subworkflow can export:
- ✅ A **single workflow**, or  
- ✅ **all workflows**, depending on configuration and permissions

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **workflowId** (Optional): 🆔 The ID of a specific workflow to export  
  *(for example: `15508756-7ee1-4c03-ad1b-d3d95aca478f`)*  
  If left empty (`""`), **all workflows** will be exported.

- **searchLimit** (Required): 🔢 The maximum number of workflows to export.

- **githubRepoName** (Required): 📁 The name of the **existing GitHub repository** to export workflows to.

- **githubOwnerName** (Required): 👤 The GitHub **owner or organization name**.

- **githubRepoBranchName** (Required): 🌿 The GitHub **branch name** to export to.

- **dynatraceGithubConnection** (Required): 🔑 The Dynatrace GitHub connection name used for authentication.

- **adminAccess** (Required): 🛡️ Boolean flag (`true` / `false`) controlling export scope:
  - `false`: Only workflows the user has permission to access will be exported  
  - `true`: If the user has **workflow-admin** rights, **all workflows** will be exported

---

### 🔗 **GitHub Connection Setup**

Before using this subworkflow, ensure that a **GitHub Connector** is configured in Dynatrace.

📖 Official Dynatrace documentation:  
https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/analyze-explore-automate/workflows/actions/github/github-workflows-setup [1](https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/analyze-explore-automate/workflows/actions/github/github-workflows-setup)

This guide explains how to:
- Configure outbound access to GitHub
- Create and authorize a GitHub connection
- Generate and scope a GitHub personal access token
- Make the connection available to workflows

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 🔍 Identify workflows based on `workflowId`, permissions, and `adminAccess`
- 📤 Export workflow definitions to the specified GitHub repository and branch
- ✅ Complete if export succeeds
- ❌ Fail if export encounters an error, allowing the **parent workflow to retry or react**

---

### 🧩 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Designed for **workflow lifecycle and governance automation**
- ✅ Enables Git-based workflow management
- ✅ Complements workflow create, delete, and validation subworkflows
- ✅ Supports automation-at-scale and CI/CD patterns

---
